verdict_reasoning: HSP60/HSPD1 is canonically a mitochondrial matrix chaperonin essential for protein folding, but is well-documented as a cell-surface 'ecto-HSP60' on stressed, apoptotic, infected, and tumor cells, where it acts as a danger signal engaging innate immune receptors. Tumor-cell surface HSP60 has been pursued as a therapeutic/diagnostic target. This matches cell_state_induced (stress/oncogenic/cell-death surface translocation of a nominally intracellular chaperone).
